description_long: This is a metallic adjustable wrench with a long, straight handle and a robust, angular head. The head features a movable lower jaw, which can be adjusted using a knurled thumbwheel to fit various sizes of nuts and bolts. The body of the wrench is solid and appears to be made from a single piece of metal, likely steel, with a dark, slightly glossy finish. The handle is flat and tapers slightly towards the end, with a hole near the tip for hanging or storage. The jaws are smooth and parallel, designed for gripping flat-sided fasteners without damaging them. There are no visible brand markings, grip patterns, or signs of rust or wear on the surface.
description: A dark metallic adjustable wrench with a smooth, solid handle and a movable jaw for gripping fasteners. The finish is glossy and the tool appears clean and well-maintained.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wrench, highlighting the straight handle and the adjustable jaw mechanism.
description_view_1: Front view focusing on the open jaws and the adjustment wheel, with the handle receding into the background.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle and the head, similar to view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the jaws and the handle's end, with the adjustment mechanism partially visible.
